purpose: Machine-readable requirements for current commercial Console objects.
machineBoundary: Current commercial business objects, route ownership, required route/action capabilities, and evidence requirements.

lifecycle.removalCondition: Replace only with a newer current contract for the same boundary.

productTruth: OPL Console / OPL Cloud Console commercial launch slice
currentSlice: Business-chain closure for account login, wallet, compute allocations, storage volumes, attachments, stable Workspace URL, Ledger entries, Fabric operations, admin audit, and support mappings.
principles: Active business object contract contains only current commercial objects.
Workspace is the stable commercial object: stable URL, access state, lifecycle, persistent StorageVolume, and current runtime pointer.
ComputeAllocation is one replaceable account-owned dedicated CVM or Kubernetes node selected from the package catalog.
StorageVolume can exist without compute; StorageAttachment mounts one volume to one current ComputeAllocation runtime.
RuntimeTemplate/ImageRef is deployable runtime configuration only; it is not a billing object, storage owner, or Workspace identity.
Destroying compute suspends the Workspace URL and retains storage; rebuilding compute reuses the same Workspace URL and StorageVolume.
Destroying storage makes the Workspace unrecoverable because the file body is gone.
Ledger owns wallet, top-up, hold, release, settlement, reconciliation, and LedgerEntry money facts.
Fabric owns provider execution evidence and FabricOperation facts.
Console owns Account, User, Session, Workspace lifecycle, AdminAuditEvent, SupportTicketMapping, and read DTO orchestration.
Frontend displays backend DTOs and must not derive pricing, billing, lifecycle, or provider state locally.
Future approval, policy, raw provider diagnostics, snapshot/recovery, payment orders, usage logs, and retired runtime lifecycle objects live outside the active contract until implemented.
repoBoundaryRules: Console owns UI, auth, route contracts, and read-model orchestration.
Fabric owns runtime, storage, connector, and agent resource execution boundaries.
Ledger owns evidence, audit, reconciliation, and review policy boundaries.

objectKinds.boundary: Billing and ownership account for users, resources, wallets, workspaces, audit, and support mappings.
Login user bound to an Account; deletion revokes sessions and preserves audit/resource evidence.
Authenticated Console session scoped to a user and account.
Account-owned dedicated CVM or Kubernetes node allocation selected from the package compute catalog; package pool identity is placement metadata, not the billable resource identity.
Account-owned storage that can exist without compute and can attach to one active ComputeAllocation at a time.
Mounts one StorageVolume to one ComputeAllocation runtime.

Stable URL subject backed by one persistent StorageVolume, managed runtime login credentials, access state, lifecycle state, and a current replaceable ComputeAllocation/StorageAttachment runtime pointer.
Account wallet DTO sourced from Ledger wallet facts.
Money fact for top-up, hold, release, settlement, reconciliation, and wallet transaction evidence.
Provider execution fact for compute, storage, attachment, runtime, and replayable resource state.
Admin/operator action fact with actor, target, result, IP, user agent, before/after, and timestamps.
Mapping from OPL account/user/workspace/resource context to external support ticket identity.
